page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual private server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU on the x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The system is provisioned with 1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core compute capabilities and overall system throughput. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves a score of 3294.99, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). However, because the instance is limited to a single shared vCPU, its multi-core stress-ng performance remains identical at 3294.99, which ranks as weak (bottom 25%). Memory performance is constrained, with cached read-only small block bandwidth at 151.27 GB/sec (bottom 10%) and uncached large block read bandwidth at 36.16 GB/sec (bottom 25%), though large block write bandwidth reaches an average tier of 29.11 GB/sec.

The server exhibits low performance in complex operations, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 2738.52 and a database operations score of 965.62, both in the bottom 10%. Redis SET operations are measured at 2.21 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is limited to 1.52 GB/sec. Given the shared CPU allocation, minimal 1.0 GB memory capacity, and low memory bandwidth, this server is best suited for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic utility scripting, low-traffic static web hosting, lightweight development environments, or simple microservices that benefit from modern AMD EPYC instruction sets but do not require sustained multi-core execution or high memory capacity.
description: The Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is an entry-level virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. While stress-ng single-core benchmarks place its raw CPU execution in the top 10% with a score of 3294.99, the single shared core limits multi-core scaling and overall system throughput. Memory bandwidth is generally low, with cached reads at 151.27 GB/sec and uncached reads at 36.16 GB/sec. PassMark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, indicating significant performance constraints for data-heavy applications. This budget-friendly instance is best suited for lightweight utility scripts, basic static web serving, and small-scale development tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring one shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd features a shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 25 GB NVMe SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server pairing a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa core with fast local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight tasks.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU with 32 MB L3 cache
1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing
Constrained memory bandwidth and database operations throughput
Best suited for lightweight scripting, static web serving, and development
